title: Pronoun number
description: Is the pronoun in this sentence singular or plural?  Use context to choose the pronoun that makes the most sense.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>Have you seen Zoila and Betsey? I can’t find  \_\_\_\_\_\_ anywhere.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence refers to two people, Zoila and Betsey, so we should use the *plural* pronoun **them**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> Making a snow angel is one of Margarita’s favorite pastimes, even though  \_\_\_\_\_\_ makes her cold.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to the act of "making a snow angel" so we should use the *singular* pronoun **it**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>Natalie balanced four plates on  \_\_\_\_\_\_ head.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence refers to "Natalie" so we should use the *singular* possessive adjective **her**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>While  \_\_\_\_\_\_ on a business trip, I ate the rest of Muneera’s carrots without her permission.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "Maneera" so we should use the *singular* possessive adjective **her**.

assessment_items.question_content:

>The mouse peeked  \_\_\_\_\_\_ head out of the cookies in the cookie jar.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "mouse" so we should use the *singular* possessive adjective **its**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>As the first student from this school to win the regional spelling bee, you should be very proud of  \_\_\_\_\_\_!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "you" so we should use the *singular* reflexive pronoun **yourself**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>Genet considered  \_\_\_\_\_\_ a good cook; she made delicious fried chicken.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "Genet" so we should use the *singular* pronoun **herself**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>I asked Aaliyah and Diane if  \_\_\_\_\_\_ had recommendations for things to do in Boise, Idaho.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence refers to two people, so we should use the *plural* pronoun **they**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> I took my  \_\_\_\_\_\_ to the library to donate it.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence uses the singular pronoun "it", so we need a singular noun phrase, like **"box of books"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_ went on the rollercoaster even though they were afraid of heights.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence uses the plural pronoun "they", so we need a plural noun phrase, like **"Muna and Jasmin".**

>Don’t worry about the   \_\_\_\_\_\_ ; they’re more afraid of you than you are of them!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey

This sentence uses the singular pronoun "it", so we need a singular noun phrase, like **"list of instructions"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_ love tomato soup; it’s our favorite food.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence uses the *plural* possessive adjective "ours", so we need a noun phrase that is plural and in the first person, like **"Ahmed, Zahida, and I".**

>When  \_\_\_\_\_\_ little, they would pretend to be airline pilots.

This sentence uses the plural pronoun "they", so we need a plural noun phrase, like **"the girls"**.

assessment_items.question_content:

> \_\_\_\_\_\_ burrowed its way through the holes in the ground.
